question_title: Will SWK's market close price on 2026-03-13 be higher than its market close price on 2026-03-04?
summary: SWK is in a 12.6% drawdown from its 52-week high with extreme negative recent momentum (-10.2% over 10 days). The conditional base rate for stocks in 10%+ drawdowns shows 53.9% positive 7-day forward returns (763 events), providing a modest mean-reversion signal. However, this is offset by the strong continued selling pressure (stock fell from $91.96 to $81.63 in 7 trading days), broader market weakness (S&P 500 down ~2%), and the failed bounce from the Feb 25 shock. The dividend record date on March 10 provides minor support. Net assessment is essentially a coin flip.

factors.description: Conditional base rate: When SWK is in 10%+ drawdown from 52w high, 7-day forward returns are positive 53.9% of the time (763 events, mean +0.61%, std 5.88%). Monte Carlo confirms ~54%.
Strong negative recent momentum: -10.2% over 10 days, -8.5% over 5 days, indicating continued selling pressure beyond typical drawdown entry
Broader market weakness: S&P 500 declining ~2% recently, suggesting systemic selling pressure not just SWK-specific
Post-shock non-recovery: The -4.5% single-day shock on Feb 25 produced only a brief bounce before continued decline from $85.26 to $81.63, suggesting structural selling
Dividend record date March 10: Could attract some income buyers ahead of ex-div date, providing minor support

reasoning:
The reviewer approves with a warning that 49% may be slightly too pessimistic given the strong 763-event base rate. The factor-implied probability of ~45% is mechanically too low because the logit arithmetic doesn't properly anchor to the base rate. The reviewer suggests 51-52%.

Let me split the difference: the base rate anchor of 53.9% is robust, but the extreme momentum and market conditions warrant a modest discount. I'll set the final estimate at 50%.
